name: Agent4Edu Generative Learner Agent Extract
population_or_context: Personalized learning simulator for intelligent education systems and adaptive practice algorithms.
sample_size: EduData comprises 18,045 time-ordered response records from 500 Chinese high school students across mathematics and physics.
methods: Extracted the generative-agent architecture, learner profile initialization, memory and reflection mechanisms, and action-module response generation.
education_context: Personalized learning and computerized adaptive testing contexts.
response_origin: LLM-powered generative agents produce learner response data initialized from real-world response patterns.
rubric_label_space: Practice response outcomes and learner behavior signals rather than one fixed rubric label space.
label_source: Learner profiles are initialized from real-world response data; generated responses come from agent action modules.
generation_model: GPT-3.5-turbo-1106 and GPT-4-turbo are used in the experiments; unless otherwise specified, the paper reports GPT-3.5-turbo as the default.
generation_method: Generative agents equipped with learner profile, memory, reflection, and action modules generate learner responses while interacting with personalized learning algorithms.
prompt_strategy: Agent state and module design condition response generation rather than a simple one-shot prompt.
evaluation_method: Comprehensive assessment of consistency and discrepancies between agent responses and human learner responses.
scorer_use: Simulation, algorithm evaluation, and personalized learning service development; not a rubric-scorer validation dataset in this baseline.
real_response_comparator: Real-world response data initializes profiles and provides the comparator for response consistency.
reported_metrics: Learner simulation is evaluated with ACC, F1-score, and ROUGE-3; the paper reports Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo) at 66.70 ACC, 79.84 F1-score, and 37.97 ROUGE-3 in Table 1.
limitations: The staged extraction uses the arXiv HTML method and experiment sections but does not stage appendix-level prompt text.
The generated learner responses are for simulator evaluation, not direct automated scorer validation.
Prompt templates and implementation details should be reviewed in the appendix before fine-grained prompt claims.
